message:
test(cli): align two robot-mode envelope checks with hd89i stdout contract

Two assertions in tests/cli_dispatch_coverage.rs still expected the
robot-mode (--json) error envelopes on STDERR, predating the
coding_agent_session_search-hd89i fix that moved them to STDOUT so
`stdout = data only` holds uniformly for success AND failure
envelopes.

- analytics_group_by_invalid_value_returns_actionable_error: invalid
  --group-by=fortnight now reports the clap "possible values" /
  "possible value" / "invalid value" message inside the wrapped JSON
  envelope on stdout. Switch the capture from output.stderr to
  output.stdout, lowercase it, and adjust the assertion body +
  failure-message format string. The failure mode exercised is
  unchanged: --json invocation, non-zero exit, enum guidance
  visible to the caller.

- analytics_rebuild_json_envelope_structure: in the branch where
  the DB is absent, the test previously required stderr to be
  non-empty and to contain "not found" / "missing" / "error". Same
  migration — capture output.stdout instead, update the empty-check
  and contains-check, and reword the failure messages from
  "diagnostic on stderr" → "envelope on stdout" to document the
  hd89i contract inline. Comments above each block cite the hd89i
  bead and explain the pre-/post-fix location so a reader grepping
  for "stderr" doesn't assume a regression.

No behavioural change under test; this is purely catching up two
assertions that still pointed at the pre-hd89i channel.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
